Description.Content: Air-resistance bike used for conditioning, intervals, and calorie-based work.
Long metal bar used for loaded strength training.
Short handheld weight used for unilateral exercises, bilateral strength work, presses, rows, curls, and accessory training.
Lightweight rope with handles used for conditioning, coordination, footwork, agility, and jumping movements such as single unders and double unders.
A kettlebell is a compact cast-iron or steel weight with a handle, used for strength, power, conditioning, and dynamic full-body movements such as swings, cleans, presses, squats, and carries.
Fixed or mounted bar used for pull-ups, hangs, and other bodyweight pulling movements.
Elastic training band used to add variable resistance, assistance, or mobility work to strength and conditioning movements.
Indoor rowing machine used for rowing distance, calorie, duration, and interval work.
Ski ergometer used for upper-body dominant conditioning, calories, distance, and interval work.
Rack used to support a loaded barbell for squats and other strength movements.
Weighted medicine ball used for wall ball shots, throws, squats, and conditioning movements.
